KIL.PATRICK, CLAYTON, MEYER & MAOOEN 
,"' 
.,)... P.;oP'ES"ONAL CORPORATION 
\ 
ATTORNEYS AT LAW 
R. J. KILPATRICK 
STERLING S. CLAYTON 
DONALD W. MEYER 
PHILIP M. MADDEN 
STEVEN A. JONES 
MONTGOMERY COLE 
SCOTT M. KOPPEL 
TERENCE KILPATRICK 
February 21, 19B6 
ftats ,~AK'~ 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
ATTN: 
Karla Wardlow 
200 PINE AVENUE, SUITE eoe 
POST OP'P'ICE BOX 2210 
LONG BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90BOI-2210 
(2131 43S-eSeS 
(2131 77S-320e 
Re: 
Campaign filing requirements -
Long Beach City Attorney 
Dear Ms. Wardlow: 
This will confirm our telephone conversation of February 20. 
John 
R. Calhoun .• the City Attorney of the City of Long Beach had filed 
for re-election in an election which was to be held on April 8, 
1986. 
No candidates filed in opposition to Mr. Calhoun and 
earlier this month, he was declared re-elected to his office and 
his name was removed from the ballot. 
BecaJse of this situation, it is my understanding that the 
candidate is not required to file the pre-election statements of 
February and March. 
Instead, the next required filing will be 
July 31, 1986. 
I would appreciate it if you would confirm my understanding on the 
enclosed copy of this letter and return it to me in the envelope 
provided. 
Thank you for your kind cooperation. 
Yours very truly, 
PMM/eb 
cc: 
John R. Calhoun 
Steve Ehren 
MEYER & MADDEN 
CONFIRMED: 
Carla Wardlow 
March 3, 1986 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices CommjssioQ. 
February 26, 1986 
Phillip Madden 
Kilpatrick, Clayton, Meyer & Madden 
P.O. Box 2210 
Long Beach, CA 
90801-2210 
Re: 
A-86-065 
Dear Mr. Madden: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
)-&u<+< f;~~ 
J~nne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322,5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate for City Attorney who was declared re-elected unopposed and removed from the ballot must file pre-election campaign statements for February and March, or if the next required filing is July 31, 1986.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC received the advice request and acknowledged it would be processed within 21 working days, but did not provide a substantive conclusion in the available document pages.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the initial inquiry letter from counsel (February 21, 1986) and FPPC's acknowledgment letter (February 26, 1986). The substantive FPPC response/conclusion is not included in the provided text - only the acknowledgment letter appears. Karla Wardlow's confirmation notation on the original letter suggests advice was eventually provided, but that letter is not in the document text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a candidate for City Attorney who was declared re-elected unopposed and removed from the ballot must file pre-election campaign statements for February and March, or if the next required filing is July 31, 1986.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC received the advice request and acknowledged it would be processed within 21 working days, but did not provide a substantive conclusion in the available document pages.

embedding.summary: A law firm requested FPPC advice regarding campaign filing requirements for an unopposed City Attorney candidate who was removed from the ballot after being declared re-elected. The FPPC acknowledged receipt of the request on February 26, 1986.
